title: ceanothus glaber
intro: Ceanothus glaber, also known as Fremont's ceanothus, is a shrub native to the western United States, specifically in the southwestern and south-central regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ceanothus glaber include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds, and internally to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation.
